---
title: "draft3"
author: "Fredy Guevara"
date: "2026-04-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Code Setup

library(tidyverse)
gamingA_raw <- read.csv("GamingStudy_data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
names(gamingA_raw)
str(gamingA_raw)
summary(gamingA_raw)
head(gamingA_raw)
```


# How is gaming intensity associated with anxiety, life satisfaction, and social anxiety?

```{r}
library(tidyverse)



# Keep variables we want
gamingA <- gamingA_raw %>%
  select(
    Hours, streams, Game, Platform, whyplay, Playstyle,
    GAD_T, SWL_T, SPIN_T,
    Narcissism, Residence, Age, Work, Degree, Gender
  ) %>%
  drop_na(Hours, GAD_T, SWL_T, SPIN_T)

# Make sure numeric columns are numeric
gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(
    Hours = as.numeric(Hours),
    streams = as.numeric(streams),
    GAD_T = as.numeric(GAD_T),
    SWL_T = as.numeric(SWL_T),
    SPIN_T = as.numeric(SPIN_T),
    Narcissism = as.numeric(Narcissism),
    Age = as.numeric(Age)
  )

# Remove impossible or extreme values if needed
gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  filter(
    Age >= 18,
    Age < 66,
    Hours >= 0,
    Hours <= 112
  )

# Create a total gaming variable
gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(total_gaming = Hours + ifelse(is.na(streams), 0, streams))

# Create gaming groups
gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(
    gaming_level = case_when(
      total_gaming < 5 ~ "Low",
      total_gaming < 15 ~ "Moderate",
      total_gaming < 30 ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ "Extreme"
    ),
    gaming_level = factor(gaming_level,
                          levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High", "Extreme"))
  )

# Check cleaned data
glimpse(gamingA)
summary(gamingA)
```

# Start of graphs

```{r}
# Gaming Hours
ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = Hours)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "gray70", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Weekly Gaming Hours",
    x = "Hours Played per Week",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```
```{r}
# Mental health groups
ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = gaming_level, y = GAD_T, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Anxiety by Gaming Level",
    x = "Gaming Level",
    y = "GAD Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# Life satisfaction
ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = gaming_level, y = SWL_T, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Life Satisfaction by Gaming Level",
    x = "Gaming Level",
    y = "SWL Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# Social anxiety
ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = gaming_level, y = SPIN_T, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Social Anxiety by Gaming Level",
    x = "Gaming Level",
    y = "SPIN Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r}
# Creating a 10 hour group
gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(hours_group = ntile(Hours, 10))

# Mena summaries 
agg <- gamingA %>%
  group_by(hours_group) %>%
  summarise(
    anxiety = mean(GAD_T, na.rm = TRUE),
    life = mean(SWL_T, na.rm = TRUE),
    social = mean(SPIN_T, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# plotting trends
ggplot(agg, aes(x = hours_group, y = anxiety)) +
  geom_col(fill = "gray60") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Anxiety by Gaming Hour Group",
    x = "Gaming Hour Group",
    y = "Average GAD Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(agg, aes(x = hours_group, y = life)) +
  geom_col(fill = "gray60") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Life Satisfaction by Gaming Hour Group",
    x = "Gaming Hour Group",
    y = "Average SWL Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
ggplot(agg, aes(x = hours_group, y = social)) +
  geom_col(fill = "gray60") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Social Anxiety by Gaming Hour Group",
    x = "Gaming Hour Group",
    y = "Average SPIN Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```
```{r}
# Residence
top_res <- gamingA %>%
  count(Residence, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 8) %>%
  pull(Residence)

gaming_res <- gamingA %>%
  filter(Residence %in% top_res)

# Using residence to compare
ggplot(gaming_res, aes(x = Residence, y = GAD_T, fill = Residence)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Anxiety by Residence",
    x = "Residence",
    y = "GAD Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Why people play
ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = whyplay, y = SWL_T, fill = whyplay)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Life Satisfaction by Motivation for Playing",
    x = "Why Play",
    y = "SWL Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Playstyle
ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = Playstyle, y = SPIN_T, fill = Playstyle)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Social Anxiety by Playstyle",
    x = "Playstyle",
    y = "SPIN Score"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Fixing playstyles to see if it makes a diffrence

gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(
    Playstyle_clean = case_when(
      
      str_detect(Playstyle, "Single") ~ "Singleplayer",
      
      str_detect(Playstyle, "offline") ~ "Offline Multiplayer",
      
      str_detect(Playstyle, "real life friends") ~ "Online with Friends",
      
      str_detect(Playstyle, "strangers") ~ "Online with Strangers",
      
      str_detect(Playstyle, "acquaintances") ~ "Online Mixed",
      
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )
ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = Playstyle_clean, fill = Playstyle_clean)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Playstyle Distribution") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = Playstyle_clean, y = SPIN_T, fill = Playstyle_clean)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Social Anxiety by Playstyle")

# Now fixing why play
gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(
    whyplay_clean = case_when(
      
      str_detect(whyplay, "fun") ~ "Fun",
      
      str_detect(whyplay, "relax") ~ "Relaxation",
      
      str_detect(whyplay, "improv") ~ "Improvement",
      
      str_detect(whyplay, "win") ~ "Competition",
      
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = whyplay_clean, fill = whyplay_clean)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Why People Play Games") +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = whyplay_clean, y = SWL_T, fill = whyplay_clean)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Life Satisfaction by Motivation")
```

```{r}
# Trying other stuff


gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(
    anxiety_per_hour = GAD_T / (Hours + 1),
    life_per_hour = SWL_T / (Hours + 1)
  )

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = gaming_level, y = anxiety_per_hour, fill = gaming_level)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Anxiety per Gaming Hour",
       x = "Gaming Level",
       y = "Anxiety per Hour") +
  theme_classic()

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = Hours, y = SWL_T, color = whyplay_clean)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(title = "Gaming Hours vs Life Satisfaction by Motivation")

gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(high_anxiety = ifelse(GAD_T >= 8, 1, 0))

gamingA %>%
  group_by(gaming_level) %>%
  summarise(rate = mean(high_anxiety)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = gaming_level, y = rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Proportion of High Anxiety by Gaming Level",
       y = "Proportion")

gamingA %>%
  group_by(Residence) %>%
  summarise(avg_anxiety = mean(GAD_T)) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_anxiety)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Residence, avg_anxiety), y = avg_anxiety)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top Countries by Anxiety")

gamingA <- gamingA %>%
  mutate(hours_group = ntile(Hours, 10))

gamingA %>%
  group_by(hours_group) %>%
  summarise(avg_life = mean(SWL_T)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hours_group, y = avg_life)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Life Satisfaction Across Gaming Intensity")

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = Playstyle_clean, y = GAD_T, fill = Playstyle_clean)) +
  geom_boxplot()

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = whyplay_clean, y = GAD_T, fill = whyplay_clean)) +
  geom_boxplot()

ggplot(gamingA, aes(x = Hours, y = SWL_T)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~ Playstyle_clean) +
  labs(title = "Gaming vs Life Satisfaction by Playstyle")
```
